document.title: Generic Piano Analytics ecommerce tracking plan
document.notes: Generic Piano Sales Insights fixture for validating ecommerce event bundles without GA4 payload placeholders.

measurement_brief.scope: Generic product add-to-cart, new cart creation, purchase confirmation, and purchased-product events.
measurement_brief.business_goal: Measure ecommerce funnel actions and product revenue attribution with Piano Sales Insights.
analysis_needs: Understand which products are added to cart
Measure cart creation and checkout completion
Tie purchased products to the transaction
constraints: Use Piano native Sales Insights event and property names
Keep cart and transaction identifiers technical and non-personal
open_questions: Confirm whether cart.creation is emitted only when the add-to-cart action creates a new cart.
Confirm the final product and cart property availability.

detected_archetypes.archetype: Retail ecommerce with Piano Sales Insights

page_roles.business_purpose: Measure add-to-cart, cart creation, transaction confirmation, and purchased products with Piano-native Sales Insights events.
page_roles.primary_success_signal: A completed transaction.confirmation event linked to product.purchased rows.

selected_event_families.reason: Piano Sales Insights provides a native product add-to-cart event with product and cart properties.
Cart creation should be captured separately when the add-to-cart action creates a new cart.
Piano Sales Insights uses transaction.confirmation for completed transaction context.
Purchased products are sent as product-level Sales Insights events linked to the transaction.

excluded_event_families.reason: This fixture is Piano-only; GA4 ecommerce parameters are not mixed into Piano Sales Insights payloads.
Piano native Sales Insights transaction and product events cover the ecommerce scenario.

events.official_match: Piano Sales Insights product add-to-cart event
events.business_question: Which products are added to cart and with which product context?
events.trigger: User confirms an add-to-cart action for a product.

platform_mappings.official_match: Piano Sales Insights product add-to-cart event
platform_mappings.implementation_notes: When this action creates a new cart, emit cart.creation as a separate event in the same user action sequence.

implementation_payloads.notes: Canonical Piano SDK payload for an add-to-cart action.

privacy.consent_dependency: analytics consent according to client privacy mode
privacy.notes: Use product and cart identifiers that are technical commerce IDs, not personal identifiers.

expected_data_layer: Not applicable when the Piano SDK is implemented directly; if GTM is used as a bridge, verify the same product properties are available before the SDK call.
expected_network: Piano collection request contains event name product.add_to_cart.
Properties product_id, cart_id, and product_quantity are populated.
qa.debugview_expectation: product.add_to_cart appears once after the add-to-cart action.

events.implementation_notes: Do not replace this Sales Insights event with a generic click event when product data is available.

events.official_match: Piano Sales Insights cart creation event
events.page_or_component: Product detail add-to-cart flow creating a cart
events.business_question: How often does add-to-cart create a new cart and what is the initial cart composition?
events.trigger: A cart is created, often immediately after the first add-to-cart action.

platform_mappings.official_match: Piano Sales Insights cart creation event
platform_mappings.implementation_notes: Emit only when a new cart is created, not on every cart update.

implementation_payloads.notes: Canonical Piano SDK payload for cart creation.

privacy.consent_dependency: analytics consent according to client privacy mode
privacy.notes: Cart IDs can be high-cardinality; keep them technical and avoid personal data.

expected_data_layer: Not applicable when the Piano SDK is implemented directly; if GTM is used as a bridge, verify cart summary properties before the SDK call.
expected_network: Piano collection request contains event name cart.creation.
Property cart_id is populated and cart_quantity equals the initial cart quantity.
qa.debugview_expectation: cart.creation appears once when the first cart is created.

events.implementation_notes: Pair with product.add_to_cart only when the cart is newly created.

events.official_match: Piano Sales Insights transaction confirmation event
events.business_question: How many transactions are confirmed and what is the order value?
events.trigger: Purchase confirmation is displayed after a successful transaction.

platform_mappings.official_match: Piano Sales Insights transaction confirmation event
platform_mappings.implementation_notes: Emit once on purchase confirmation; pair with one product.purchased event per purchased product line.

implementation_payloads.notes: Canonical Piano SDK payload for a transaction confirmation.

privacy.consent_dependency: analytics consent according to client privacy mode
privacy.notes: Transaction IDs are high-cardinality technical commerce IDs; do not include personal or billing data.

steps: Display the purchase confirmation page once.
expected_data_layer: Not applicable when the Piano SDK is implemented directly; if GTM is used as a bridge, verify transaction summary properties before the SDK call.
expected_network: Piano collection request contains event name transaction.confirmation.
Property transaction_id is populated and event fires once per confirmed transaction.
qa.debugview_expectation: transaction.confirmation appears once on the confirmation page.

events.implementation_notes: Keep this as the transaction-level confirmation event; product-level purchased details belong in product.purchased.

events.official_match: Piano Sales Insights purchased product event
events.page_or_component: Checkout confirmation page purchased product list
events.business_question: Which products are purchased within each transaction?
events.trigger: Purchase confirmation is displayed and purchased product lines are available.

platform_mappings.official_match: Piano Sales Insights purchased product event
platform_mappings.implementation_notes: Emit for purchased product lines so transaction-level and product-level analysis remain separate.

implementation_payloads.notes: Canonical Piano SDK payload for a purchased product line.

privacy.consent_dependency: analytics consent according to client privacy mode
privacy.notes: Use controlled product taxonomy and technical IDs only.

steps: Complete a generic checkout journey with one product.
Display the purchase confirmation page once.
expected_data_layer: Not applicable when the Piano SDK is implemented directly; if GTM is used as a bridge, verify purchased product properties before the SDK call.
expected_network: Piano collection request contains event name product.purchased.
Properties product_id and transaction_id are populated for the purchased product line.
qa.debugview_expectation: product.purchased appears with the transaction_id that matches transaction.confirmation.

events.implementation_notes: For multiple products, repeat this event per product line or follow the client's agreed Piano Sales Insights batching pattern.

parameters.reporting_purpose: Identifies products consistently across Piano Sales Insights ecommerce events.
parameters.value_rules: Use the stable product SKU or product catalog identifier.

parameters.reporting_purpose: Connects Piano cart lifecycle events to the same cart instance.
parameters.value_rules: Use a technical cart identifier that contains no personal data.

parameters.description: Piano Sales Insights transaction identifier for confirmation and purchased-product events.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Connects Piano transaction and product purchase events and supports deduplication.
parameters.value_rules: Use a technical transaction identifier that contains no personal data.

parameters.description: Piano Sales Insights product name property.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Provides readable Piano product labels for ecommerce reporting.
parameters.value_rules: Use a controlled product label or catalog product name; avoid personal or free-text values.

parameters.description: Piano Sales Insights product variant property.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Segments Piano product performance by variant or option.
parameters.value_rules: Use lowercase ASCII snake_case controlled values when possible.

parameters.description: Piano Sales Insights product unit price including tax.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Supports Piano revenue and product price analysis using tax-included price.
parameters.value_rules: Use a decimal number in the cart currency.

parameters.description: Piano Sales Insights product quantity property.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Supports cart and purchase quantity analysis in Piano ecommerce reporting.

parameters.description: Flag indicating whether the add-to-cart action created a new cart.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Identifies whether a product action created a new cart in Piano Sales Insights.
parameters.value_rules: Use true only when a new cart is created by the action.

parameters.description: Currency used for cart and transaction monetary values.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Supports Piano Analytics reporting with the platform-native property model.

parameters.description: Cart or transaction value including tax.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Supports Piano Analytics reporting with the platform-native property model.
parameters.value_rules: Use a decimal number in the cart currency.

parameters.description: Total quantity of products in the cart.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Supports Piano cart-size analysis across checkout progression.

parameters.description: Number of distinct products in the cart.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Supports Piano Analytics reporting with the platform-native property model.

parameters.description: Payment method category for the transaction.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Supports Piano Analytics reporting with the platform-native property model.
parameters.value_rules: Use controlled lowercase ASCII snake_case categories.

key_events.reason: Primary ecommerce conversion in Piano Sales Insights.
key_events.conditions: Mark as conversion according to the Piano workspace measurement strategy when appropriate.

not_tracked.interaction: Generic add-to-cart click without product properties
not_tracked.reason: A Sales Insights product.add_to_cart event is more useful and official when product properties are available.
Free-text error messages can contain sensitive information and are not required for the generic ecommerce fixture.

assumptions: Piano Analytics JavaScript SDK is the implementation method.
Piano Sales Insights is enabled for ecommerce reporting.
No GA4 stream is in scope for this generic fixture.

documentation_sources_checked.name: Piano Analytics Sales Insights
documentation_sources_checked.checked_for: product.add_to_cart, cart.creation, transaction.confirmation, product.purchased, and required Sales Insights properties

expected_data_layer: Not applicable when the Piano SDK is implemented directly; if GTM is used as a bridge, verify product properties before the SDK call.
expected_network: Piano collection request contains event name product.add_to_cart.
Properties product_id, cart_id, and product_quantity are populated.
qa_cases.debugview_expectation: product.add_to_cart appears once after the add-to-cart action.

expected_data_layer: Not applicable when the Piano SDK is implemented directly; if GTM is used as a bridge, verify cart summary properties before the SDK call.
expected_network: Piano collection request contains event name cart.creation.
Property cart_id is populated and cart_quantity equals the initial cart quantity.
qa_cases.debugview_expectation: cart.creation appears once when the first cart is created.

steps: Display the purchase confirmation page once.
expected_data_layer: Not applicable when the Piano SDK is implemented directly; if GTM is used as a bridge, verify transaction summary properties before the SDK call.
expected_network: Piano collection request contains event name transaction.confirmation.
Property transaction_id is populated and event fires once per confirmed transaction.
qa_cases.debugview_expectation: transaction.confirmation appears once on the confirmation page.

steps: Complete a generic checkout journey with one product.
Display the purchase confirmation page once.
expected_data_layer: Not applicable when the Piano SDK is implemented directly; if GTM is used as a bridge, verify purchased product properties before the SDK call.
expected_network: Piano collection request contains event name product.purchased.
Properties product_id and transaction_id are populated for the purchased product line.
qa_cases.debugview_expectation: product.purchased appears with the transaction_id that matches transaction.confirmation.
